Degradation of PEVA films due to environmental stressors like humidity, temperature and solar radiation in the field aged modules is not uncommon.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["According to the general consensus within the PV industry, PEVA as an encapsulant should have VA content b etween 28 and 33%.", "PEVA films containing 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content with t he necessary additives have been fabricated and cured at 150 \u00b0C under vacuum and subsequently subjected to accelerated damp -heat (85\u25e6C and 85% RH) aging according to IEC 61215 standard for ", "Tensile strength, thermal stability and degree of crosslinking have been found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) due to DH aging.", "However, inadequate % transmittance and relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH a ging discourages PEVA18 as an encapsulant.", "Moreover, PEVAs with higher VA content (33 and 40%) have been degraded significantly after 2000 h of DH aging r esulting in significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: According to the general consensus within the PV industry, PEVA as an encapsulant should have VA content b etween 28 and"}]}
